title: diplolaena ferruginea
intro: Diplolaena ferruginea, also known as the Red Ironbark or Rough-leaved Diplolaena, is a plant species native to Australia. It is a shrub or small tree with a reddish-brown trunk and rough, leathery leaves. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions. Diplolaena ferruginea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in some modern herbal remedies to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
